title.zh: 冬季养生：温阳补肾，藏精养神
title.en: Winter Wellness: Warm Yang, Tonify Kidney, Store Essence

description.zh: 冬季寒冷，如何通过饮食温补、穴位保健和生活习惯温阳补肾，增强体质抵御寒冬
description.en: Learn how to warm Yang and tonify kidney through diet, acupoints, and lifestyle during cold winter

zh.text: 冬季是一年中最冷的季节，阳气潜藏。中医认为
，冬季养生重在温阳补肾、藏精养神，为来年春天的生发积蓄能量。
肾为先天之本，冬季是补肾的最佳时节。肾气充足，则身体强健，抵抗力强。

items: 多吃温补肾阳食物：羊肉、核桃、黑豆、黑芝麻、韭菜
适当食用滋补肾阴食材：枸杞、山药、桑葚
注意保暖，尤其是腰部和足部

zh.text: 冬季阳气潜藏，人体新陈代谢减缓，是养精蓄锐的好时机。

items: 早睡晚起，保证充足睡眠
减少剧烈运动，以静养为主
保持心情平和，避免情绪波动
适度室内活动，如太极、八段锦

zh.text: 位置：背部第二腰椎棘突下旁开1.5寸。
位置：足底，蜷足时足前部凹陷处。

zh.title: 羊肉当归汤
zh.instructions:
1. 羊肉切块焯水去腥
2. 加入当归、生姜炖煮
3. 煮1小时后加入枸杞
4. 温服，每周2-3次
5. 温补肾阳，增强体质

zh.text: 温馨提示：冬季进补要因人而异，阴虚火旺者不宜过度温补。如有慢性疾病，请咨询专业医师。

en.text: Winter is the coldest season, when Yang energy hides deep. TCM believes 'Winter governs storage', so winter wellness focuses on warming Yang, tonifying kidney, and storing essence for spring's renewal.
The kidney is the foundation of life. Winter is the best time to tonify the kidney. Strong kidney Qi means strong body and immunity.

items: Eat Yang-warming foods: Lamb, walnut, black beans, black sesame, chives
Include kidney-Yin nourishing ingredients: Goji berries, yam, mulberry
Stay warm, especially lower back and feet

en.text: Yang energy hides in winter, metabolism slows. This is an ideal time to accumulate energy.

items: Early to bed, late to rise, ensure sufficient sleep
Reduce intense exercise, focus on quiet cultivation
Moderate indoor activities: Tai Chi, Ba Duan Jin

en.text: Point 1: Shenshu (BL23) - Tonifies kidney, strengthens lower back
Location: Back, 1.5 cun lateral to 2nd lumbar vertebra.
Point 3: Yongquan (KI1) - Tonifies kidney, secures essence
Location: Sole of foot, depression when toes curled.

en.title: Lamb & Angelica Soup
en.instructions:
1. Cut lamb, blanch to remove odor
2. Add angelica and ginger, simmer
3. After 1 hour, add goji berries
4. Serve warm, 2-3 times weekly
5. Warms kidney Yang, strengthens body

en.text: Note: Winter tonification should be personalized. Those with Yin deficiency and excess fire should avoid excessive warming. Consult a physician for chronic conditions.
